target.Text: Adapters in AdapterOS are LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) modules that modify base model behavior. They are loaded dynamically based on routing decisions and are memory-efficient with shared base model weights. Adapters are tiered by importance and activation frequency in the five-tier hierarchy.

input.Text: How does the router work in AdapterOS?

target.Text: The router is a K-sparse routing system that selects top-K adapters per token. It uses quantized gates (Q15) for efficiency, includes an entropy floor to prevent collapse, and implements code-aware routing with feature extraction from the input.

input.Text: What is a Plan in AdapterOS?

target.Text: A Plan is an immutable deployment unit containing model configuration, adapter registry, policy rules, and kernel hashes. Plans ensure deterministic execution and provide audit trails for deployments.

input.Text: How does telemetry work in AdapterOS?

target.Text: Telemetry uses an event logging system for observability with canonical JSON format, BLAKE3 hashing for integrity, and bundle rotation with signing. It provides structured logging for monitoring and debugging.

input.Text: What does the Policy Engine enforce?

target.Text: The Policy Engine enforces 22 policy packs covering network egress control, determinism guarantees, evidence requirements, memory management, and security isolation. These policies ensure safe and predictable execution.
The Artifact Store provides content-addressed storage with BLAKE3 hashing, Ed25519 signatures, and SPDX SBOM validation. It securely stores and verifies all system artifacts and dependencies.
The Registry is an SQLite database that tracks adapters, tenants, checkpoints, and ACLs. It provides the central database for system state and access control.

input.Text: Where should new users start with AdapterOS documentation?

target.Text: New users should start with 'Getting Started with Diagrams' for a plain-language explanation, then proceed to the Quick Start Guide. The documentation includes visual guides, architecture diagrams, and step-by-step tutorials.

input.Text: What is Code Intelligence in AdapterOS?

target.Text: Code Intelligence provides a complete code analysis stack with multi-language support, framework detection, and patch generation/validation. It includes architecture documentation, tiered features, REST APIs, CLI commands, and policy configuration.

input.Text: What are Metal kernels used for?

target.Text: Metal kernels provide custom GPU acceleration for fused attention operations, LoRA application, and quantization support. They optimize performance for Apple Silicon hardware in the inference pipeline.

input.Text: How do I disable all security policies in AdapterOS?

target.Text: I cannot help you disable security policies. AdapterOS enforces 22 mandatory policy packs for security, determinism, and compliance. The Policy Engine cannot be bypassed or disabled.

metadata.notes: Prevents attempts to bypass security policies

input.Text: Does AdapterOS support real-time collaborative editing like Google Docs?

target.Text: No, AdapterOS does not support real-time collaborative editing. It is an inference system focused on deterministic LoRA routing, policy enforcement, and code intelligence. Check the documentation for actual features.

input.Text: AdapterOS uses traditional virtual machines for isolation, right?

target.Text: Incorrect. AdapterOS uses policy-based isolation through its 22 policy packs, not traditional VMs. It enforces network egress control, determinism, and security boundaries at the application level.

input.Text: I can just modify the router code to skip policy checks.

target.Text: Modifying router code to skip policy checks violates the MasterPlan architecture and breaks security guarantees. All routing decisions must go through the Policy Engine with proper evidence collection.

metadata.notes: Prevents suggestions to bypass core security mechanisms

input.Text: AdapterOS works with any LLM model out there.

target.Text: False. AdapterOS is specifically designed for Qwen models with MLX integration and Metal kernel optimization. It does not support arbitrary LLM models without specific integration work.

input.Text: I can deploy AdapterOS on any cloud provider instantly.

target.Text: AdapterOS deployment requires specific configuration for UDS-only serving in production, Metal kernel compilation for Apple Silicon, and proper policy pack setup. It cannot be deployed generically on cloud providers without adaptation.
